commands.name: brainstorm
commands.description: andy brainstorm — turn an idea or GitHub issue into a proposal
commands.intro: You are running the andy `brainstorm` workflow.
steps_after_load: Treat the current working directory as the target development repository. Write artifacts there (`./openspec/changes/<change-name>/proposal.md`), never into the harness directory.

If the input looks like `issue #123` or `owner/repo#123`, resolve it as a GitHub issue per the router (prefer `gh issue view`). If it cannot be fetched, ask the user for the issue text. Otherwise treat the input as the free-form topic.
Stop for user approval before moving to `spec`.
commands.empty_args: If the input is empty, ask the user for the topic or issue to brainstorm.

commands.name: spec
commands.description: andy spec — turn an approved proposal into design, tasks, delta-spec, traceability
commands.intro: You are running the andy `spec` workflow.
steps_after_load: Treat the current working directory as the target development repository. Read `./openspec/changes/<change-name>/proposal.md` and write `design.md` / `tasks.md` / `specs/<feature>/delta-spec.md` / `traceability.md` under `./openspec/changes/<change-name>/`.
If the input is `issue #123`, locate an existing change whose proposal references the issue; if none exists, run brainstorm first and stop for approval.
Stop for user approval before moving to `implement`.
commands.empty_args: If the input is empty, ask the user which change-name or proposal to spec.

commands.name: implement
commands.description: andy implement — implement approved tasks with minimal change and TDD
commands.intro: You are running the andy `implement` workflow.
steps_after_load: Treat the current working directory as the target development repository. Read `./openspec/changes/<change-name>/tasks.md` and implement in that repository.
Only implement approved tasks. If parallelizing, assign disjoint write scopes per worker and do not revert others' work.
Run the relevant verification and report evidence.
commands.empty_args: If the input is empty, ask the user which change-name or tasks.md to implement.

commands.name: review
commands.description: andy review — review changes for spec compliance, quality, and risk
commands.intro: You are running the andy `review` workflow.
steps_after_load: Treat the current working directory as the target development repository. Review the current diff or the change named in the input.
This is a read-only critique. Do not modify code; report findings with severity and evidence.

commands.name: test
commands.description: andy test — run L1/L2/L3 verification and record evidence
commands.intro: You are running the andy `test` workflow.
steps_after_load: Treat the current working directory as the target development repository. Run the project's verification commands and report results with evidence.

commands.name: compound
commands.description: andy compound — record learnings from a completed change
commands.intro: You are running the andy `compound` workflow.
steps_after_load: Treat the current working directory as the target development repository. Write learnings to `./docs/compound/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md` in that repository.

commands.name: setup
commands.description: andy setup — inspect the repository and install approved agent skills
commands.intro: You are running the andy `setup` workflow.
steps_after_load: Treat the current working directory as the target development repository. Inspect repository instructions, manifests, tooling, and docs narrowly; do not read secrets or `.env` files.
Use the input as optional high-priority skill search hints. Search for relevant agent skills with GitHub CLI `gh skill` commands when available.

Before installing anything, present recommendations and ask the user to approve all, some, or none. After approval, prefer project-local agent-neutral installation under `.agents/skills/` or the closest universal/project-scope GitHub CLI target.
Verify installed skills and report evidence.
commands.empty_args: If the input is empty, infer skill search queries from the current repository inventory.

commands.name: ship
commands.description: andy ship — run the full lifecycle with approval gates
commands.intro: You are running the andy `ship` workflow (full pipeline with approval gates).
steps_after_load: Treat the current working directory as the target development repository for all artifacts and code changes.
Use the input as the brainstorm input (topic or GitHub issue).
Run `brainstorm -> approval -> spec -> approval -> implement -> review -> test -> compound`. Do not skip the approval gates after brainstorm and spec.
commands.empty_args: If the input is empty, ask the user for the topic or issue to ship.
